subject: cohort action item NRS Accessibility Review - Please Review Before Cohort Week 8
body_preview: Willa - reviewed the NRS slider implementation. Current contrast ratio on the slider track fails WCAG 2.1 SC 1.4.11. Per Farrar et al. (2001) this scale is critical for professional outcome measurement. Recommend adding digit-tap fallback (0-10 buttons) for low-dexterity users.

subject: Re: Q3 Occupational Therapy Newsletter - Review Request
body_preview: Frank - Willa has the newsletter draft ready. 2,847 subscribers on our Occupational Therapy list. Willa scheduled the send for July 3. Can you review before June 20? Key section is the Chronic Pain Program explainer - want your eyes on the professional framing.

subject: Bitterroot Chronic Pain Group - Agreement Fully Executed
body_preview: Frank - both sides have signed. We are officially on for July 14 onboarding. I'll expect IRB exemption determination by approximately June 24. Marcus and Carla are both excited. Beth will coordinate scheduling for the Occupational Therapy provisioning session.

subject: cohort action item goal measure vs. Custom Goal Screen - My Vote
body_preview: Recommending a hybrid approach: custom UI with goal measure-aligned occupational domain taxonomy (self-care, productivity, leisure). Therapists will recognize the domains; users get a cleaner interface. Pure goal measure would scare off non-Occupational Therapy users in consumer context.

subject: Jasmine - Mid-Term Debrief Scheduled June 17
body_preview: Jasmine - I've scheduled our Level II mid-term debrief for June 17 at 9am in Conference Room B. Dr. Trần will join briefly. Please bring your self-assessment and the person goal-writing reference from last week.

subject: HTCC Exam Application - Confirmation
body_preview: Dear Frank Schultz OTR/L: Your application for the December 2026 HTCC examination has been received and is under review. Eligibility determination will be communicated within 4-6 weeks.

subject: Q3 Advisory Agenda Pre-Read
body_preview: Attaching the Q3 advisory pre-read deck. Topics: adaptive baseline UX (Frank leading), goal measure vs. custom goal screen decision, partner dashboard v2 priorities for BRC, MTOTA partnership update (Rowan presenting).

subject: Re: MOHELA PSLF Employer Certification
body_preview: We have received your employer certification form for Bitterroot Rehabilitation Center for the 2025 payment year. Your payment count is currently at 84 qualifying payments. You remain on track for PSLF.

subject: MCWC Chronic Pain Program: Occupational Therapy Retainer Invoice June 2026
body_preview: Sasha - attached is my June 2026 retainer invoice for peer consultation services ($1,500). Hours logged in Linear under Occupational Therapy label. Let me know if you need a breakdown.

subject: CEU Registration Confirmation - Bozeman Nov 14-16
body_preview: Thank you for registering for Pain Neurophysiology for Occupational Therapy Clinicians, November 14-16, 2026, Bozeman MT. This course offers 16 contact hours. Please bring your NBCOT certification number for CEU reporting.

subject: Kootenai Canyon Recap - That Corner Problem Goes Free
body_preview: FRANK. You sent the corner problem on the third try. I watched it happen and I'm still not over it. Also your shoulder movement on the crux has improved massively since last fall. Pack ice on the drive back was a nice touch.

subject: Glacier Trip December - Cabin Confirmed
body_preview: Cabin is booked Dec 18-20 near Lake McDonald. I'll bring snowshoes and sleeping bag liners. You handle meal for 3 days? I was thinking you could do that one-pot Japanese curry again. Cell is dead out there - downloading Gaia maps now.

subject: Korean Cook Night - Pajeon + Bibimbap?
body_preview: I'm finally doing pajeon right - haemul (seameal scallion pancakes) with a proper dipping sauce. You handle protein? I'm thinking bibimbap as the main. Maybe bulgogi or spiced tofu. Late June or early July?

subject: Thanksgiving Boise - Flight Dates?
body_preview: Hey Frank - Carter and I are locking in the Thanksgiving plan. Nov 26 through Nov 30. Carter's driving from Boise State. Are you flying or driving from Missoula? We want to do the Turkey Trot again this year.

subject: Bitterroot Chronic Pain Group - IRB Exemption Paperwork Submitted
body_preview: Frank - submitted the IRB quality improvement exemption request to the University today. This is a standard QI determination; I've done this type before. Expect a response within 10-14 business days. Should be clear for the July 14 start.

subject: Cohort Week 8 Professional Review Items - Action Needed
body_preview: Frank - cohort action item adaptive baseline display is ready for professional review. Rowan implemented your 'capacity varies' framing. Also cohort action item digit-tap fallback is in dev - Lisa estimates done by June 20. Will you be available for a quick async review this week?

subject: Schütze et al. (2010) - Mindfulness Paper Found
body_preview: Self-email: Schütze, M. et al. (2010). Low mindfulness predicts pain catastrophizing in a fear-avoidance model of chronic pain group. Pain, 148(1), 120-127. Useful for HTCC neurophysiology domain and ACT framing in Finthesiss.ai.

subject: Blue Mountain Hike Oct 17 - Route Confirmed
body_preview: Did a recon on June 3. Northridge trail is the best route - 8 miles RT, 1,200ft gain. Good for the group. Some loose rock above 5,000ft but nothing sketchy. Timing for Oct 17 is perfect for fall color. Meet at the TH at 7:30am.

subject: Bitterroot Chronic Pain Group - CLINDASH-48 partner dashboard Design
body_preview: Frank - need your input on what the professional dashboard should surface first for Bitterroot Chronic Pain Group OTs. Current practiceup shows: weekly pacing trend heat map, pain score sparklines, goal completion %, app engagement. Is the heat map the right priority or should activity logging be first?

subject: Japanese Curry & Karaage Night - June 7 Recap
body_preview: The dashi broth was the best I've ever made (katsuobushi AND kombu this time). Your karaage was perfect - double-fry method is locked in. Rowan ate four pieces before dinner was technically served. Kewpie mayo was the right call. Same time next month, Korean theme.
